component.name: Wire-Payment Estate + Canadian Domestic Rails (Reference Model)
component.description: Cryptographic dependency map across the cross-border wire-payment lifecycle (pain.001 -> orchestration -> sanctions screening -> SWIFT messaging MT103 / pacs.008 -> HSM signing -> RTGS settlement pacs.009 -> archive), extended with two Canadian domestic settlement endpoints: Lynx (Payments Canada RTGS, over SWIFTNet) and the Real-Time Rail (RTR, Payments Canada, ISO 20022 over JSON/REST). Each asset carries a DOCUMENTED / INFERRED confidence label.

authors.name: Muhammad Atif — Payments PQC Migration Analysis

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Signature forgery once a CRQC exists (integrity/authenticity)

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model signing primitive; RSA-2048 is quantum-vulnerable per NIST.

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Wraps AES data-encryption keys for long-term archive

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later on archived payment data & PII

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model key transport; RSA-OAEP is quantum-vulnerable.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Signature forgery / impersonation once a CRQC exists

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model EC signature; quantum-vulnerable per NIST.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL: recorded TLS sessions decrypted post-Q-day

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Grover only — collision resistance mildly reduced

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Retain; prefer SHA-384 for new high-assurance use

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SHA-2 is standard across SWIFT/ISO 20022; Grover-only, quantum-resistant.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Grover halves effective strength -> still 128-bit; safe

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Integrity between back-office app and SWIFT interface

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Deploy (hybrid with existing signer during transition)

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: NIST FIPS 204 standard; Cyber Centre ITSP.40.111 recommends ML-DSA.

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: NIST FIPS 203 standard; Cyber Centre ITSP.40.111 recommends ML-KEM.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Shor breaks RSA — CA compromise / trust-chain forgery

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFT documents the SWIFTNet PKI CA root as 4096-bit RSA; managed by SWIFT, not the participant.

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: PQC signature for CA roots / long-lived trust anchors (FIPS 204)

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Deploy for CA roots and long-validity certificates

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: NIST FIPS 204 highest security category; appropriate for long-lived roots per ITSP.40.111.

properties.name: payments-pqc:lifecycle-stage
properties.value: RTR: API payload signing over ISO 20022 JSON

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Shor breaks the underlying RSA/ECDSA signing key — payload forgery

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: RTR Participation Guide (Oct 2025) states payloads must be 'encrypted, digitally signed and validated', but the specific JWS algorithm (RS256/ES256) is NOT public — it sits in the participant-only RTR Exchange API spec.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL via ECDHE; legacy version also lacks modern hygiene

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model internal mesh; standard TLS 1.3 configuration.

components.name: Lynx Transport — IPsec VPN (SWIFT Alliance Connect)

properties.name: payments-pqc:lifecycle-stage
properties.value: Lynx: SWIFTNet transport over Secure IP Network

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Carries Lynx MX messages (pacs.008/009) over SWIFTNet InterAct

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL on ECDH/RSA key establishment; AES-256 bulk cipher is quantum-safe

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFT documents Alliance Connect IPsec with AES + RSA-2048 auth over the Secure IP Network; exact negotiated cipher suite for a given Lynx participant is INFERRED.

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Browser access to Lynx operational GUI

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL on handshake key exchange & certificate authentication

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: ML-KEM-768 hybrid key exchange + ML-DSA certs

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: TLS for the admin GUI is standard practice; specific version/cipher suite is not published by Payments Canada.

cipherSuites.name: IPsec site-to-site tunnel (RSA/ECDH auth, AES bulk)

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Carries RTR REST/JSON payment exchange traffic

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL on key establishment; AES bulk cipher quantum-safe

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: RTR Participation Guide confirms payment-exchange APIs run over a site-to-site VPN / IPsec tunnel; the negotiated cipher suite is INFERRED.

properties.name: payments-pqc:lifecycle-stage
properties.value: RTR: portal access & token-based API authorization

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: HTTPS portal + OAuth 2.0 token-authorized APIs via IDAM

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL on TLS key exchange; OAuth token-signing keys (likely RSA/ECDSA) quantum-vulnerable

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: ML-KEM hybrid TLS + ML-DSA token signing

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: DOCUMENTED that RTR uses TLS/HTTPS, OAuth 2.0 'where possible', and IDAM tokens; TLS version and token-signing algorithm are INFERRED.

components.name: RTR Reporting — SFTP over SSH (public-key auth)

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: SSH host/user keys (RSA/ECDSA/Ed25519) quantum-vulnerable; HNDL on SSH kex

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: PQC SSH — ML-KEM key exchange, ML-DSA host keys

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: RTR Participation Guide states SFTP reporting requires SSH public-key authentication; specific key algorithm is INFERRED.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: RSA-2048 subject key & signature both quantum-vulnerable

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Re-issue on ML-DSA-65; hybrid cert during transition

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFTNet subscriber certificates are 2048-bit RSA (SWIFT PKI).

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: ECDSA signature & EC public key quantum-vulnerable

properties.name: payments-pqc:lifecycle-stage
properties.value: Lynx: CAD domestic RTGS message signing

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Signs Lynx pacs.008 / pacs.009 / pacs.004 over SWIFTNet InterAct

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: RSA-2048 subject key & signature both quantum-vulnerable

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Re-issue on ML-DSA-65; hybrid cert during transition

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFTNet subscriber certificates are 2048-bit RSA (SWIFT PKI). That this Lynx participant uses a standard SWIFTNet business certificate is INFERRED from Lynx running over SWIFTNet InterAct.

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFTNet PKI 2048-bit RSA in FIPS 140-2 L3 HSM (platform-derived).

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Signs liquidity transfers (the Project Leap touchpoint)

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model RTGS signing key; the Project Leap touchpoint.

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Wraps all payment signing keys at rest

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: FIPS 140-2 L3 HSM master key; AES-256 quantum-resistant.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: AES safe, BUT the RSA-2048 key-wrap = HNDL exposure

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Re-wrap DEKs under ML-KEM-768; this is the HNDL crux

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: Reference-model archive key-wrap; illustrative of standard envelope encryption.

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Signs Lynx settlement messages (proof of origin / non-repudiation)

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Forgery of Lynx settlement authorisation post-Q-day

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: ML-DSA-65 key pair (payload ~13x larger than RSA-2048 sig)

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFTNet PKI uses 2048-bit RSA keys in FIPS 140-2 L3 HSMs; Lynx TSP-005 confirms signature verification ('verified with decrypted signature'). Key size is platform-derived (SWIFT), not restated by Payments Canada.

securedBy.mechanism: SWIFT HSM hardware root of trust (FIPS 140-2 L3)

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Protects Lynx RSA signing keys at rest

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Grover only — AES-256 remains safe; but HSM firmware must gain PQC support

properties.name: payments-pqc:target-algorithm
properties.value: Retain AES-256; migrate to PQC-capable HSM (FIPS 140-3 L3 + PQC pending)

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: SWIFT HSM standard is FIPS 140-2 L3; no FIPS 140-3 L3 CMVP validation covering PQC inside the boundary exists as of early 2026 (possibly 2027) — an external dependency.

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: Forgery of real-time payment instructions post-Q-day

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: DOCUMENTED that RTR payloads are signed; the key algorithm, size, and HSM custody are INFERRED from Vocalink/Interac platform precedent (UK Faster Payments, TCH RTP) and are gated in the participant-only RTR Exchange API spec.

properties.name: payments-pqc:lifecycle-stage
properties.value: RTR: National Payment Fraud Services API

properties.name: payments-pqc:touchpoint
properties.value: Authenticates the mandatory Central Fraud Analytics score call per payment

properties.name: payments-pqc:threat
properties.value: HNDL + authentication forgery on the fraud-scoring API channel

properties.name: payments-pqc:confidence-note
properties.value: DOCUMENTED that NPFS (Central Fraud Analytics, Central Risk List, Confirmation of Payee) is mandatory and API-delivered; the channel's crypto is INFERRED.

dependencies.ref: lifecycle/6b. RTR — Real-Time Rail (pacs.008, ISO 20022/JSON) [part INFERRED]
